summary: Updated GALE01 symbol metadata after running a modified decomp-toolkit string detector. The PR only changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt, converting many previously generic object symbols into data:string entries and adjusting their sizes to the detected string length rather than the padded object span. The body links the dtk patch used, but the slice has no review discussion.
what_changed: Changed one file, config/GALE01/symbols.txt, with 1786 additions and 1786 deletions across 98 hunks.

Added data:string annotations to many object symbols that had previously lacked a data type, including examples such as lbShadow_803BA0D8, lb_803BA1C0, lbHeap_803BA3D0, lbArchive_803BA588, lbSnap_803BACF4, str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported, str_assert_jobj_flags_JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION, and many *_ssm and *_hps path/name strings.

Adjusted object sizes downward where the string detector identified the true null-terminated string length inside a padded allocation, for example lbShadow_803BA0D8 changed from size 0xC to 0xB data:string, lb_803BA1C0 from 0x10 to 0xE data:string, lbHeap_803BA3DC from 0x24 to 0x21 data:string, and str_main_ssm from 0xC to 0x9 data:string.
Preserved existing symbol addresses, scopes, and names while changing metadata comments; there is no evidence in the slice of C source, assembly, or linker script changes.
The PR body explicitly cites the modified dtk patch used: https://github.com/ribbanya/decomp-toolkit/commit/f62e589f2cb4a91115bb18809918a7a7b29ac8bc.
smart_moves: Used tooling to identify strings systematically instead of hand-editing scattered symbol metadata.
Kept the update constrained to the generated symbol configuration file, making the change easy to audit as a metadata-only improvement.
Retained padded addresses and neighboring symbol layout while marking only the actual string payload size, which helps distinguish string contents from alignment or following data.
Linked the exact decomp-toolkit patch in the PR body, giving future maintainers a reproducible lead for how the detections were produced.
decomp_lessons: When updating symbols.txt for detected strings, mark the object with data:string and use the actual string byte length including the terminator when that differs from the padded allocation span.
Large symbol metadata churn can still be low-risk if addresses, scopes, and symbol names are preserved and only type/size comments change.
String detection is useful beyond obvious str_* locals; global address-named symbols such as lbHeap_803BA3D0 or lb_803BA8D8 may also be strings.
For future decomp work, better string metadata can improve data declarations and reduce ambiguity between byte arrays, 4-byte data, jump tables, and true C strings.
If a PR depends on non-upstream tooling behavior, include the exact tool patch or commit so later contributors can reproduce or validate the generated output.
naming_conventions: Existing address-based symbol names were preserved, e.g. lbShadow_803BA0D8, lbHeap_803BA3D0, lbArchive_803BA588, lbSnap_803BACF4.

Existing local string-style names were preserved where present, including str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported, str_assert_jobj_flags_JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION, str_LbBf_dat, str_struct_lbBgFlashColAnimData_NULL, str_lbRefSetUnuse_error, and str_lbrefract_c.
The diff shows many file/resource-name strings using suffixes such as *_ssm and *_hps, for example str_main_ssm, str_nr_title_ssm, str_falco_ssm, str_castle_hps, and str_ff_mario_hps.
No evidence in the slice indicates that this PR renamed symbols; it mainly refined data type and size metadata.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The matching tactic was metadata refinement: classifying objects as data:string so generated data declarations can better represent the original binary.
The repeated size reductions suggest the tool detected null-terminated strings inside padded object slots; future similar changes should avoid treating the padding as part of the string.
The PR used a modified decomp-toolkit/dtk string detection pass rather than manual per-symbol inference.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided slice.
No reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; the merge happened shortly after creation.
searchable_terms: Detect more strings using modified dtk
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that mass-update config/GALE01/symbols.txt with data:string annotations.
Look up the linked decomp-toolkit patch f62e589f2cb4a91115bb18809918a7a7b29ac8bc to understand the modified string detection rule.
Search for symbols whose size changed from padded 0xC/0x10/0x18 spans to shorter data:string lengths.
Check whether later decomp PRs relied on these *_ssm or *_hps string annotations for matching data declarations.
